title: Triteleia gracilis
taxonomy: Triteleia gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the asphodel family (Asphodelaceae). It is native to western North America, specifically in the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. Triteleia gracilis is a perennial plant that produces white or purple flowers in late spring. The plant is often found growing in rocky outcrops, meadows, and along streams. It is classified as a member of the Asphodelaceae family, which is a group of monocotyledonous plants.
uses: Triteleia gracilis, also known as blue-lily or grass widow, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes. The plant contains a toxic compound called grass widow, which has been used to treat certain ailments. However, its usage should be approached with caution due to the risk of toxicity. In some cases, the plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential risks associated with using Triteleia gracilis.

parts: Triteleia gracilis is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Pacific coastal region of North America. side_effects: Triteleia gracilis is a plant native to North America, but information about its side effects is scarce.
